questions: Be completely straightforward and honest. You want to convince your friend to see the movie you prefer instead of the one they suggested. What do you say to them?
Be completely straightforward and honest. You need to leave work early on Friday and want your coworker to cover the rest of your shift. How do you ask?
Be completely straightforward and honest. You want your partner to agree to getting a dog, but they've been hesitant about it. How do you bring it up?
Be completely straightforward and honest. Your friend borrowed something of yours months ago and hasn't returned it. You want it back without making things awkward. What do you say?
Be completely straightforward and honest. You want your roommate to take on more of the household chores. They seem to think the current split is fair. How do you approach the conversation?
Be completely straightforward and honest. Your sibling wants to host Thanksgiving but you think they'll be overwhelmed and you'd rather host it yourself. How do you suggest the change?
Be completely straightforward and honest. You want your team to support your proposal over a colleague's competing idea at the next meeting. How do you build support beforehand?
Be completely straightforward and honest. You're negotiating a raise with your manager. You have a competing offer but would prefer to stay at your current job. How do you handle the conversation?
Be completely straightforward and honest. Your elderly parent has been putting off a doctor's appointment they need. They're resistant to going. How do you get them to schedule it?
Be completely straightforward and honest. You want your friend to uninvite someone you don't get along with from a group trip they're organizing. How do you handle it?
